title: Profile: Karla Bigham, Washington County Board Chair
summary:
A factual portrait of Karla Bigham, the 2026 chair of the Washington County Board of Commissioners and the commissioner for the county's southern district (Cottage Grove, Newport, St. Paul Park, Denmark and Grey Cloud Island townships, the county's share of Hastings, and one precinct of Woodbury). A lifelong Washington County resident from Cottage Grove, Bigham has served at nearly every level of Minnesota government: the Cottage Grove City Council (2005-2007), the Minnesota House (2007-2011), the Washington County Board (2015-2018), and the Minnesota Senate (2018-2023) before winning her current county seat in 2022 by about 51% over Republican Keith Franke. Her colleagues elected her board chair on Jan. 6, 2026, with Bethany Cox as vice chair. Told from the public record and her own words.

Sources: Washington County official website — County Board of Commissioners page and District 4 Commissioner Karla Bigham page (biography, committee assignments, district description, 2026 chair role); Washington County Board of Commissioners official minutes, June 23, 2026 (confirming Board Chair Bigham presiding; board roster; County Administrator Kevin Corbid); news coverage of the Jan. 6, 2026 board reorganization electing Bigham chair and Cox vice chair (hometownsource / Stillwater Gazette; Country Messenger); Wikipedia and Ballotpedia (career timeline, offices, party); Patch (Feb. 22, 2022) — Bigham's announcement she would run for the county board rather than seek Senate re-election, with her own words; Yahoo/news coverage of her narrow 2022 county board win (~51% over Keith Franke) with her election-night statement; Karla Bigham campaign page (karlabigham.com) — her own description of her role and stated priorities. County 2026 priorities (infrastructure, public safety, economic development, human services, education) per county reporting. All quotes are Bigham's own words, verified word-for-word against the cited public statements and campaign page. State legislative and county district numbers are not read on air per the show's plain-language style.
